old_claim.note: Sociology (Early) is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim.note: Mass Media (Newspapers) is the earlier print-mass-audience channel in the mass-media lineage; sociology is a field that studies media rather than a prerequisite medium.

source_shape.source_locator: Definition and print-media sections: identify print media including newspapers as mass media, note newspapers developed into mass-audience media in the 19th century, and list newspapers with radio and television among major mass-media forms.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source places newspapers in the lineage of mass media alongside radio and television.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The corrected edge represents a predecessor medium, while sociology is a later analytic field that can study mass media without enabling it.

ontology_before: Mass Media depended on Sociology (Early), making a field of study look like a prerequisite medium.
ontology_after: Mass Media depends on radio broadcasting, television, printing press, and Mass Media (Newspapers) as media-channel lineage.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: mass_media now depends on mass_media_newspapers and no longer directly depends on sociology_early.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped to the academic study of mass media rather than mass communication technologies.
A source shows early sociology was necessary to create mass-media channels.
mass_media_newspapers is removed without adding a narrower print-mass-media predecessor.
short_reason: Newspapers are a predecessor mass medium; sociology is a study field.
